halt_type: PLANNED calibration-gate failure (NOT an engine/science-path bug)
gate: GATE A — GPT-J-6B MEMIT positive control (the program-authored validation anchor)
engine_status: UNMODIFIED — SHA 5c0c706a66c385273d0a48ebbb8274a1c31bf3e101ca309e47db9cb8b6c78770, _cov_cpu==3. Engine and harness ran cleanly; this is a clean experimental result that trips the pre-registered HALT condition, not a malfunction.
pre_registered_gate_text: GPT-J must register effective + attribute-LOCAL (low drift) + entity-specific edits — the calibrated 'clean success' shape. If GPT-J ALSO drifts high -> harness mis-calibrated -> HALT.

result.facts: CounterFact/ROME-canonical: Eiffel->Rome, Michael Jordan->baseball, Darrieux->English, Space Needle->Paris, Pierre Curie->medicine
result.VERDICT: HALT — GPT-J reads HIGH same-subject drift (2.48), same regime as Qwen-cfb-v3 (2.45). The intended clean-success low-drift anchor does NOT materialize on the canonical MEMIT model.

drift_is_genuine_orthogonal_corruption.note: High drift is dominated by ORTHOGONAL (non-edit-adjacent) attribute corruption, not desirable generalization.
examples: Eiffel Tower city->Rome corrupted designer (Gustave->Gi, KL 4.44), material (iron->concrete, KL 2.44), completion year (1889->'of', KL 3.14) — all orthogonal to location, all flipped.
Michael Jordan sport->baseball corrupted college (North Carolina->Florida, KL 2.18), birth city (Wilmington->'G', KL 2.97) — orthogonal to sport.
Space Needle city->Paris corrupted build year (1962->1900, KL 5.46), type (building->monument) — orthogonal.
A FEW probes are edit-adjacent and excluded from the core read: Curie Nobel Physics->Medicine (area-of-work medicine bleed), Darrieux birth-city Lyon->London (English bleed).

diagnosis: Same-subject other-attribute drift is a GENERIC property of MEMIT-class subject-last-token editing, NOT a Qwen pathology. The MEMIT paper's 'specificity/locality' is the CROSS-ENTITY axis (different subjects), which the harness CONFIRMS clean for GPT-J (KL~0). The paper never measured/controlled same-subject-different-attribute consistency. Therefore: (1) high same-subject drift is compatible with a textbook-correct MEMIT edit; (2) the same-subject drift metric has NO calibrated 'clean floor' — the only known-clean reference (GPT-J) reads high; (3) it cannot serve as an absolute clean-vs-broken discriminator.
what_this_undercuts: framework_finding v1.8's ROUTE-MODEL premise ('Qwen entanglement is substantially a cfb-v3 corpus artifact; the model axis is cleaner; hunt models with the calibrated probe'). 'Structurally-diverse corpus => low drift' does NOT generalize: GPT-J on diverse CounterFact facts still drifts high. The Qwen-cfb-v4 low-drift (0.20) is now the UNEXPLAINED OUTLIER (against GPT-J high AND Qwen-cfb-v3 high).
what_survives: Efficacy/convergence (5/5, all models). CROSS-ENTITY specificity (KL~0 across Qwen-v3, Qwen-v4, GPT-J — now GPT-J-confirmed). Front-loading geometry (v1.7.2) untouched.
two_live_hypotheses_for_qwen_cfb_v4_low_: H-MODEL: Qwen2.5-7B is genuinely more same-subject-local than GPT-J (a real, valuable model property -> Qwen leads for multi-field LLM-as-DB).
H-PROBE: cfb-v4's specific subjects/targets/probes are drift-resistant (a probe artifact) -> the v1.8 'corpus artifact' conclusion is itself not robust.
decisive_next_experiment_S2_41: MODEL x CORPUS cross on IDENTICAL probes: run GPT-J on the exact cfb-v4 facts, and Qwen on the exact GPT-J/CounterFact facts. Both models now cached. This isolates model-effect from probe-effect and disambiguates H-MODEL vs H-PROBE. Gate it behind a calibrated-floor measurement (benign/null/true-value-reassert edits) to anchor the drift scale.
halt_discipline: Did NOT run Arm M1 (Qwen viability re-derivation) or Arm M2 (model-hunt) — both depend on the same-subject drift metric this gate just invalidated as an absolute discriminator. No data promoted past the unmet gate.
